name: Evert Latin
classification_notes: The foundry traces Evert's lineage to Miller and Richard's Grotesque No.1, Roger Excoffon's Antique Olive and Evert Bloemsma's FF Balance, placing it firmly in the humanist grotesque tradition with contemporary and experimental inflections.
summary: A 48-style grotesque by Kostas Bartsokas, drawing on Miller and Richard's Grotesque No.1, Antique Olive and FF Balance across three optical sizes and a variable axis.
description: Released in 2024 and awarded a TDC Certificate of Typographic Excellence in 2025, this family spans three optical sizes — Text, standard and Display — each available in eight weights with matching italics and a variable font. The design blends the structural rigidity of early Victorian grotesques with the humanistic warmth associated with Excoffon and Bloemsma, resulting in a voice that sits between editorial and expressive work. Latin coverage extends to 328 languages, with Greek support and OpenType features including old-style numerals, tabular figures and discretionary ligatures.
foundry_blurb: Foundry Five describe Evert as a typeface that moves across early grotesques, mechanical tradition and contemporary modernity, with its genetic makeup rooted in Miller and Richard's Grotesque No.1, Antique Olive and FF Balance. The result, they say, is a blend of subtle rigidity and humanistic elegance — 48 styles across three optical sizes (Text, Display and a middle-ground option), supporting Latin and Greek across 328 languages, with OpenType extras including localised forms, old-style numerals and discretionary ligatures.

name: Averta PE
classification_notes: Designed in the tradition of constructed grotesques from early twentieth-century Central Europe, using monolinear strokes and clear geometric forms, with open apertures and humanist touches in the italics.
summary: A geometric grotesque in three widths and nine weights by Kostas Bartsokas, covering Latin, Greek, Cyrillic and Vietnamese across 54 static styles and a variable font.
description: Built on monolinear strokes and clear geometric forms, Averta PE draws on the constructed grotesque tradition of early twentieth-century Central Europe, with open apertures and alternate characters borrowed from Anglo-American gothics adding warmth to the upright styles. The italics deliberately avoid compass-and-ruler geometry, introducing a humanist quality that distinguishes them from the roman. Cyrillic support earned third place at the 2017 Granshan Awards, and the family includes localised forms for Bulgarian, Macedonian, Russian and Serbian.
foundry_blurb: Averta is a geometric grotesque designed by Kostas Bartsokas, its name taken from the Greek word meaning to speak openly and without reservation. The five middle weights were refined specifically for small-size legibility, while the lightest and heaviest weights are optimised for headlines. Available as 54 static styles across three widths and nine weights with matching italics, plus a variable font, it supports over 450 languages across Latin, Greek, Vietnamese and award-winning Cyrillic, with localised letterforms for Bulgarian, Macedonian, Russian and Serbian.

name: Evert PE
classification_notes: Evert PE draws from early grotesque traditions, citing Miller and Richard's Grotesque No.1, Antique Olive and FF Balance as influences, resulting in a humanist-inflected grotesque with experimental touches.
summary: A 48-style grotesque by Kostas Bartsokas spanning three optical sizes, drawing on Miller and Richard's Grotesque No.1, Antique Olive and FF Balance to produce a typeface with both rigidity and humanistic warmth.
description: Designed by Kostas Bartsokas and released in 2024, Evert PE covers Latin, Greek and Cyrillic across 381 languages in three optical sizes: Text, a plain intermediate and Display. Eight weights run from Thin to Black with matching italics, and the family is also available as a variable font. It earned a TDC Certificate of Typographic Excellence in 2025 and a merit from Communication Arts the same year, with OpenType features including old-style numerals, tabular figures and discretionary ligatures.
foundry_blurb: Foundry Five describe Evert PE as a typeface that blends the essence of Miller and Richard's Grotesque No.1 with inspiration from Roger Excoffon's Antique Olive and Evert Bloemsma's FF Balance, creating a mix of subtle rigidity and humanistic elegance. It comes in 48 styles across three optical sizes, supports Latin, Greek and Cyrillic with 381 languages, and includes a variable font alongside OpenType features such as localised forms, old-style numerals and discretionary ligatures.

name: Jali Arabic
classification_notes: Jali Arabic is a humanist sans serif with low-contrast strokes, ample counters and distinguishable letterforms, designed specifically for wayfinding and multi-script harmony across Arabic, Latin and Greek.
summary: A humanist sans designed for wayfinding, combining Arabic, Latin and Greek in a single family that maintains a native voice across all three scripts.
description: Designed by Mohamad Dakak and originating as a graduation project, Jali Arabic addresses the shortage of Arabic typefaces suited to both design quality and readability. Low-contrast strokes, generous counters and carefully differentiated marks give the family clarity at signage sizes and in demanding typographic environments. The name means 'clear' in Arabic, and the typeface won a TDC Certificate of Typographic Excellence and Granshan first prize in the Arabic and Latin category in 2019.
foundry_blurb: Foundry Five describe Jali as a humanist sans serif particularly suited for wayfinding, combining Arabic, Greek and Latin in harmony while preserving a native voice for each script. The design began as a graduation project motivated by a lack of Arabic typefaces that meet both design and readability requirements, and was later expanded to reach its full potential across all supported scripts. Eight styles are available in desktop, web, app and ebook formats.

name: Jali Latin
classification_notes: Jali is explicitly described as a humanist sans serif, with low-contrast strokes, ample counters and a warm, legible voice suited to wayfinding and demanding typographic environments.
summary: A humanist sans designed for wayfinding, combining Latin, Arabic and Greek scripts in a single unified family with a clear, warm voice across 16 styles.
description: Designed by Mohamad Dakak and reviewed by Gerry Leonidas, Jali began as a graduation project addressing the shortage of readable Arabic typefaces before expanding into a full multi-script family. Low-contrast strokes, ample counters and carefully distinguished letterforms make it well suited to signage, transport environments and demanding editorial settings. The Latin cut covers 332 languages across 16 upright and italic styles, and the family earned a TDC Certificate in Typographic Excellence and a Granshan 1st Prize, both in 2019.
foundry_blurb: Foundry Five describe Jali as a humanist sans particularly suited to wayfinding, combining Arabic, Greek and Latin in harmony while keeping a native voice for each script. The name means 'clear' in Arabic, reflecting the design's ethos of low-contrast strokes, ample counters and distinguishable marks that together offer a warm and efficient reading experience.

name: Jali Greek
classification_notes: Jali Greek is a humanist sans serif with low-contrast strokes and ample counters, designed with clarity and warmth as guiding principles, consistent with humanist sans classification.
summary: A humanist sans designed for wayfinding and demanding typographic environments, covering Greek and Latin with a clear, warm voice across 16 styles.
description: Designed by Mohamad Dakak with the Greek drawn by Kostas Bartsokas, Jali Greek is a wayfinding-oriented humanist sans that balances functional clarity with a natural typographic voice. Low-contrast strokes, generous counters and carefully distinguished letterforms make it well-suited for signage, transport and any setting where legibility under pressure matters. The family won a first prize at Granshan 2019 and an EBGE award for original Greek typeface design in 2022.
foundry_blurb: Foundry Five describe Jali Greek as a humanist sans serif particularly suited for wayfinding, combining Greek and Latin in harmony while maintaining a native voice for each script. The name Jali means 'clear' in Arabic, reflecting the typeface's ethos of low-contrast strokes, ample counters and distinguishable marks for a warm and efficient reading experience. The family spans 16 styles across upright and italic cuts, and supports 334 languages.

name: Peridot PE
classification_notes: A sans-serif type system with humanist characteristics, offering broad width and weight ranges with full Cyrillic, Greek and Latin support, consistent with a contemporary humanist approach to multi-script type design.
summary: A 120-style sans-serif superfamily by Pria Ravichandran and Kostas Bartsokas, spanning six widths and ten weights with full Latin, Cyrillic and Greek coverage across 379 languages.
description: Named after the olivine mineral found in Lanzarote's lava fields, Peridot PE is a humanist sans-serif system built across six widths from Compressed to Extended, each with ten weights and matching italics. Latin was designed by Pria Ravichandran and Kostas Bartsokas, with Cyrillic and Greek by Bartsokas alone, covering localised variants and typographic niceties including discretionary ligatures, fractions and tabular currencies. The family won an Award of Excellence at Communication Arts Typography Competition 2024 and an original typeface award at the Greek Communication Design Awards 2023.
foundry_blurb: Foundry Five describe Peridot PE as the gem of their library, named after the sparkling greenish-yellow olivine mineral found in Lanzarote's black basalt lava fields. They position it as a high-quality 10-weight family with matching italics across 6 widths, available in variable format, supporting Cyrillic, Greek and Latin across over 370 languages. The family comprises 120 static fonts and is designed for use across branding, display, editorial, advertising, web and screen contexts.

name: Peridot Latin
classification_notes: A multi-width sans serif with humanist proportions and warmth, avoiding strict geometric or grotesque rigidity. The range of widths and stylistic variants points to a flexible humanist-influenced system.
summary: A 120-style sans serif system by Pria Ravichandran and Kostas Bartsokas, spanning six widths and ten weights with full variable font support and coverage of over 320 Latin languages.
description: Named after the olivine mineral found in Lanzarote's lava fields, Peridot Latin is a thoroughly developed sans serif type system released in 2022 by Foundry Five. Six width variants run from Compressed to Extended, each with ten weights and matching italics, amounting to 120 static styles plus variable fonts. Vietnamese and over 320 languages are supported, alongside tabular figures, currency symbols, fractions and discretionary ligatures, making it viable across branding, editorial, advertising and screen contexts.
foundry_blurb: Foundry Five describe Peridot Latin as a multifaceted sans serif type system crafted with passion and precision, developed through long hours with a keen focus on detail. The family spans 120 static fonts across six widths and ten weights with matching italics, available in variable format. It covers over 320 languages including Vietnamese and includes localised variants, tabular numerals, currencies, fractions and discretionary ligatures, making it suited to branding, display, corporate, editorial, advertising, poster, web and screen use. It received the EBGE Original Typeface award at the Greek Communication Design Awards 2023 and an Award of Excellence at Communication Arts Typography Competition 2024.

name: Peridot Devanagari
classification_notes: A multi-script sans serif system with humanist proportions, designed to work cohesively across Devanagari and Latin scripts with a full weight and width range.
summary: A 10-weight, 6-width Devanagari and Latin sans serif system with variable support, covering over 330 languages and awarded at Communication Arts Typography 2024.
description: Designed by Pria Ravichandran with Latin support from Kostas Bartsokas, Peridot Devanagari is a substantial multi-script type system spanning 20 static styles across 10 weights with matching italics and six widths, all available in variable format. The family supports Devanagari and Latin across more than 330 languages, with localised variants, tabular numerals, fractions and discretionary ligatures built in. It received the Award of Excellence at the Communication Arts Typography Competition 2024 and an EBGE award for original typeface at the Greek Communication Design Awards 2023.
foundry_blurb: Foundry Five describe Peridot Devanagari as a high-quality 10-weight sans serif type system with matching italics in six widths and variable format support, covering Devanagari and Latin across more than 330 languages. It includes localised variants, tabular numerals, currencies, fractions and discretionary ligatures, and is suited to branding, editorial, advertising, display, web and screen use. The family comprises 20 static fonts plus variable support, and is available in OTF, TTF and WOFF2 formats.

name: Peridot Tamil
classification_notes: A multi-script sans serif system with humanist proportions, designed to function across Tamil and Latin scripts with extensive weight and width range. The Tamil script was designed specifically by Pria Ravichandran.
summary: A 20-style Tamil and Latin sans serif system with variable support, covering 314 languages across 10 weights and six widths.
description: Designed by Pria Ravichandran with Latin contributions from Kostas Bartsokas, Peridot Tamil pairs Tamil and Latin scripts in a single cohesive system. The family spans 10 weights with matching italics, six width variants and full variable format support, making it suitable for branding, editorial, advertising and screen work. It was awarded the EBGE Original Typeface award at the Greek Communication Design Awards 2023 and an Award of Excellence at Communication Arts Typography Competition 2024.
foundry_blurb: Foundry Five describe Peridot Tamil as part of a multifaceted sans serif type system named after the greenish-yellow mineral Olivine found in Lanzarote's lava fields. The Tamil variant supports Tamil and Latin scripts across 314 languages, includes localised variants, tabular numerals, currencies, fractions and discretionary ligatures, and is available in 20 static fonts plus variable format. They position it for use across branding, display, editorial, advertising, poster, web and screen applications.
